A NWS STORM SURVEY INDICATED THAT AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON THE WEST SIDE OF FIRESIDE LAKE, ON MUD LAKE ROAD. THE TORNADO TRACKED APPROXIMATELY 7.7 MILES NORTHEAST, BEFORE LIFTING ON THE EAST SIDE OF THE FLAMBEAU RIVER, WEST OF SHEEP CAMP ROAD. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 14 MINUTES, WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 100 YARDS AND MAXIMUM WIND SPEED OF 85 MPH. NUMEROUS TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN OR UPROOTED. A FEW TREES LANDED ON LAKE CABINS BUT NO INJURIES WERE REPORTED.
